text:
Once upon a time in a small village tucked between trees, there was a curious fox with a talent for weaving strange webs.

Fred thought the webs would make excellent socks, but alas, they were way too fuzzy. Instead, he used them to decorate his den with intricate patterns.

One chilly evening, as he dozed off in his nest, he dreamed about talking trees. He called Oak-tree Felix.

"Fred, you've got a knack for this crazy craft! People love your patterns," said Felix one moonlit night.

"My goodness, Felix! How can I make more?"

"You've got to listen to the wind. It whispers secrets."

On Fred's next weaving day, he sat under the willows. The chatter was like gossiping fairies.

"But isn't it boring?" Fred demanded.

"No darling, it's music."

So Fred let the wind guide him. It told him tales, jokes on old gnarled trees, and dreams where squirrels became dragons.

At dawn
